See the thoroughly documented history in [[crystal detector]]

Reverted [[WP:AGF|good faith]] edits by [[Special:Contributions/JoeNMLC|JoeNMLC]] ([[User talk:JoeNMLC|talk]]): Not a type of diode.
/* Types of semiconductor diode */ ce, add s.a. Transistor diode model
/* Main functions */ clarify that the bridge rectifier is actually four diodes.
/* Threshold voltage */ adding "turn-on" as another name
/* Threshold voltage */ really out to specify the germanium and silicon as "pn" to distinguish from "Schottky" one listed which is also "Silicon"

/* Threshold voltage */ traced https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Siirded.png in Inkscape and cleaned up a bit and only use (positive) forward current and voltage for simplicity and made into an .svg to compare these four common diodes (added Infrared GaAs to the table too).
/* Operating regions */ forgot minus sign before Is, cause is a negative current.

/* Operating regions */ Deleting "Thus, if an external voltage greater than and opposite to the built-in voltage is applied, a current will flow" because that is not actually what the diode law says. Doesn't have to be greater than built-in voltage...adding {{See also|P–n diode#Forward bias}} instead, which explains: the forward current increases exponentially with forward bias voltage due to the exponential increase in carrier densities, so there is always some current at even very small values
/* Operating regions */ Move stuff on "knee" to be earlier when talk about threshold voltage section.  Cause there really isn't one.
/* Threshold voltage */ the exponential curve not really having a "threshold" needed a bit more explanation and needed a corresponding graph to look at showing the linear scale vs semi-log scale.

Reverting possible vandalism by [[Special:Contribs/2603:800C:15F0:A510:A4C3:A837:DCBD:BF1B|2603:800C:15F0:A510:A4C3:A837:DCBD:BF1B]] to version by FrescoBot. [[WP:CBFP|Report False Positive?]] Thanks, [[WP:CBNG|ClueBot NG]]. (4269451) (Bot)
Alter: template type. Add: s2cid. | [[:en:WP:UCB|Use this bot]]. [[:en:WP:DBUG|Report bugs]]. | #UCB_CommandLine 269/7965
/* top */ genericize hatnote, WikiNav shows practically nobody clicks on this anyway
intro is not very short, criticism needs to be more specific

/* Operating regions */ use bullet list instead of numbered list for these 4 regions, because the numbered list used "1" for both the first and last region, like "1", "2", "3", "1".  Anyway bullet is fine because don't need to give these regions numbers.
/* Small-signal behavior */ this is explained int he Shockey diode article
/* Operating regions */ remove not needed word.
/* Operating regions */ rearrange a bit to clarify more about why called "threshold" or "cut-in" or "knee".
/* Operating regions */ normal instead of "typical" here because "typical" is used later as a specified term in spec sheets.
/* Operating regions */ added citation to an adafruit learning article that says that 20 mA and 25 C is a typical spec point for LEDs.
/* Operating regions */ hyperlink "knee" to [[Knee of a curve]] so users can be familiar with this concept.
/* Operating regions */ try to clarify about the usage of the loose terms "voltage drop" and "forward voltage".
/* Operating regions */ slight clarify about datasheet term VF
/* Shockley diode equation */ at least point out that it is an exponential relationship.

/* Shockley diode equation */ deleting almost all of this section expect for a brief one-sentence intro.  All of what was written is also in the [[Shockley diode equation]] article, so I'm telling readers to visit that article for details.
/* Forward threshold voltage for various semiconductors */ the slink I added to rectifier voltage drop has a bit of relevant discussion on this, so adding a "See also" to it.

/* Operating regions */ clarify what "ideal" diode equation it is, by spelling out [[Shockley diode equation]] hyperlinked.  (many diode introductions treat a step-response on-off diode as the "ideal" diode, which causes confusion with the word "ideal").
/* Computing exponentials & logarithms */ tweaked my wording.
/* Computing exponentials & logarithms */ adjust grammar of my new section's name

/* Applications */ moved the logarithm example from where is was out-of-place to belong here in Applications section, along with its inverse of exponentiation.  (Note: temperature sensing is already talked about in Applications).
/* Types of semiconductor diode */ I think this section is a somewhat more appropriate place for that image I deleted a couple edits ago, because the image shows curves for several types of diodes.
/* Operating regions */ More referring to the modelling of this leveling off region, more than just an unspecified "the diode".

/* Operating regions */ clarify this "ideal" diode is referring to a "Shockley ideal diode", because "ideal" diode can have different meanings (e.g. this is not the perfect step-response "ideal" diode).

/* Operating regions */ I am removing the image [[File:Forward and Reverse Characteristics for diodes-en.svg]] because it is *way* to busy and hard to follow (has like 6 different devices and like a dozen numbered circles), and including it here makes it hard to refer to "the picture" (or "the curve" or "the graph") from this text.  Maybe someone can find another place to put that image, though.
/* Operating regions */ the picture uses lower-case V_d, not V_D, apparently.
/* Operating regions */ reads better to say "breakdown" instead of "PIV".

/* Forward threshold voltage for various semiconductors */ Instead of [[citation needed]], lets just add a section link to [[Light-emitting_diode_physics#Materials]] which list all material/colors with voltages.
/* Forward threshold voltage for various semiconductors */ format consistently
/* Forward threshold voltage for various semiconductors */ [[Schottky diode]] article says is "150–450 mV", So I'm using that range instead.
/* Forward bias */ Can't refer to pictures by direction in wikipedia, so say "its current-voltage curve".

/* Forward bias */ so many alternative names for forward threshold voltage. Collecting all names in the "Forward biased" section for now, and using "Forward threshold voltage" inthe name of this section.
/* Logic gates */ try to better express the bit about full logic with inversion
/* Logic gates */ better express the distinction between diode-resistor logic and full logic with diode-transistor logic.
keep mention of logic short in lead, cause this article doesn't talk much about logic gates.  In Logic gates section, mention the two types (diode-resistor and diode-transistor logic).
specify two forms of diode logic
added "logic" as a use (linked to [[diode logic]]).

Quickly mentioning that semiconductor has exponential current-voltage characteristic to lede, cause it's curve is a key point talked about a lot in this article and there was a heading "the lead section may be too short to adequately summarize the key points".

Introduce thermionic diode by saying "The now-obsolete..." and put it after the paragraph on semiconductor diode, which is by-far more commonly used and is by-far what people think of when they hear the word "diode", so it should be mentioned first.
/* Operating regions */ identify the "knee" at VD so readers not familiar with the concept will get it when "knee" is invoked in the list.
/* Operating regions */ simplify wording to introduce this list of regions
/* Operating regions */ can say graph will always appear on the right with wikipedia. the graph is now titled current voltage characteristic anyway.
/* Operating regions */ introduce "knee" first so makes sense why called "knee voltage".
/* Operating regions */ clarifications/improvements.  Clearly label these regions with a *bolded* name and identify these regions in the image caption.  Added other common names for the knee voltage.
/* Operating regions */ define PIV with parenthesis.
/* Operating regions */ say that this list is in order from lower to higher voltages
/* Current–voltage characteristic */ moved IV graphs to "Operating regions" section where they can be better referred from.
/* Forward bias */ clarify this bit is "for simplicity" and break off as separate paragraph.

/* Forward bias */ collect specific numbers for forward voltage drops into this new subsection "Forward voltage drops for various semiconductors" as a list.  Add new subsection heading "Four operating regions" to group the numbered list of the four operating regions.
/* Forward bias */ fix broken subsection hyperlink
